# Extractors

Parent: [Src](../README.md)

Source module for the Extractors feature area.

## Key Files

- `declarative.rs`: Grammar-driven phase-2 extractor (TOML configs in `configs/`). Ruby additionally lowers expression IR (`ruby_ir` submodule: interpolation → `template`, hash reads → `member`, assignments → `bind`/`callee`/`args`, `param_names`).
- `go.rs`: Source file in this module. Canonicalizes `fmt.Sprintf` (%s/%v) and provably-string `+` chains to `template` IR; literal-string map indexes to `member` IR.
- `grammars.rs`: Source file in this module.
- `import_helper.rs`: Source file in this module.
- `ir.rs`: Language-agnostic IR helpers — property assembly (`expr`/`bind`/`callee`/`args`/`param_names`) plus the shared canonicalizations (`concat_chain_to_template`, `is_plain_field_name`).
- `java.rs`: Source file in this module. Canonicalizes provably-string `+` concat to `template` IR and `x.get("field")` Map reads to `member` IR.
- `kotlin.rs`: Source file in this module.
- `lean.rs`: Source file in this module.
- `mod.rs`: Source file in this module.
- `python.rs`: Source file in this module.
- `rust_lang.rs`: Source file in this module.
- `typescript.rs`: Type definitions and shared contracts. The reference emitter for the property-carried expression IR (see `ir.rs`).
- ...and 1 more source files.

## Property-carried expression IR (cross-language contract)

Orchestration-mode extractors attach the SAME JSON IR properties regardless
of source language, so downstream walkers (gnexec, `gg-walker-wasm`)
evaluate without re-parsing source: `param_names` on Entry nodes, `expr` on
returns/statements, `bind` + `callee` + `args` on call statements. Language
idioms canonicalize to shared shapes — TS templates, Python f-strings, Ruby
interpolation, Go `fmt.Sprintf`/`+`, and Java `+` concat all lower to
`template` IR; TS/Python attribute reads, Go/Ruby literal-string indexes,
and Java `Map.get("field")` all lower to `member` IR. Fixture matrix +
Tier-C parity proof: `tests/gg_embed_correctness.rs` over
`gnode/benchmarks/echo.{ts,py,go,rb,java}`.

## Usage

Use this README as the local map for this directory. Keep implementation details near the owning source files, and update this document when the directory contract changes.

## Verification

Run the closest package or app-level check through the repository-owned `a0` or `monster` target after changing behavior in this directory.
